metadata.claim_text: Present mechanism, alignment, radial nerve exam, brachial pulse, splint plan, and operative versus nonoperative pathway.

metadata.action: Escalate to attending for exploration versus observation strategy with repeat neuro exam
metadata.trigger: New radial nerve deficit after closed reduction or Holstein-Lewis pattern with progressive deficit

metadata.description: Wrist drop or posterior interosseous dysfunction after humeral shaft injury.
Failure of humeral diaphyseal fracture healing.
Spiral distal-third humeral shaft fracture pattern associated with radial nerve injury.
Intramedullary nailing for unstable humeral shaft fracture patterns.

relationship_metadata.note: operative strategy and nerve exploration attending-dependent

metadata.description: Diaphyseal humerus fracture with radial nerve risk in spiral distal-third patterns.
Open plating for humeral shaft fractures when nailing is unsuitable.

metadata.claim_text: Missing a new radial nerve deficit after closed reduction of a humeral shaft fracture.
Document wrist extension and finger extension at presentation and after reduction of humeral shaft fractures.
Intramedullary nailing is a common operative answer for unstable humeral shaft fracture patterns.
Holstein-Lewis spiral distal-third humeral shaft fractures are classic radial nerve injury associations on boards.

metadata.action: Proceed with operative stabilization pathway (typically IM nail when suitable) with documented nerve exam
metadata.trigger: Unstable humeral shaft alignment, open fracture, or pathologic pattern needing rigid fixation
